commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh You are my hiding place; you shall preserve me from trouble; you shall surround me about with songs of deliverance. Augustine of Hippo 7. "Thou art my refuge from the pressures, which have compassed me about:" Thou art my refuge from the pressure of my sins, which hath compassed my heart. "O Thou, my Rejoicing, deliver me from them that compass me about" (ver. 7): in Thee is my joy: deliver me from the sorrow which my sins bring upon me.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Refuge. Hebrew, "hiding "(Psalm xxx. 21.; Haydock) or asylum. (Calmet)
Which. Hebrew, "thou shalt surround me with songs of deliverance. Sela. "Or "my praise saving, thou wilt environ me always. "(St. Jerome) (Haydock)
Perhaps th may now occupy the place of m, as the Greeks all agree; and the sense is at least the same. (Berthier)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
